name: Hideo Kojima's portrayal of cities in Policenauts
creator: Hideo Kojima
practice: Space colony urban design as a mirror to Earth's cities
approach: A colony in space replicates every urban problem: crime, corruption, pharmaceutical exploitation, surveillance. The physical infrastructure is perfect. The social infrastructure fails in identical ways. Technology changes the container but not the content.
body_of_work: Beyond Colony — a space station that reproduces Los Angeles's social pathology in a designed environment. Shows that planning perfect infrastructure doesn't solve human problems.
why_instructive: Demonstrates that sphere layers 1-6 (the AI-fillable infrastructure layers) are necessary but not sufficient. Without layers 7-10 (human design), a technically perfect place still fails.

dome_relevance.2: Systems that work technically but fail socially
dome_relevance.5: Housing that is adequate by metrics but alienating by experience

sphere_relevance.2: Perfect infrastructure doesn't equal good place
sphere_relevance.5: Social layer reflects power structures regardless of physical design
sphere_relevance.7: The absence of meaningful activation in a 'perfect' environment
